title: olearia viscosa
intro: Olearia viscosa, also known as sticky tree or sticky daisy, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its small white flowers and sticky, resinous leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of terpenoids and other compounds. The health benefits of Olearia viscosa include the potential treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be made into a tea or infused oil, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
